things.name: HEART Herning Museum of Contemporary Art
things.description: Striking museum designed by Steven Holl with outstanding collection of Danish and international modern art including works by Asger Jorn

things.name: Birk Centerpark
things.description: Cultural campus designed by C.F. Moller architects housing HEART museum, the Jutland Art Fund and outdoor sculpture park in a unique arts quarter

things.name: Herning Museum
things.description: Regional museum charting the social history of Herning from the wild heathland of the 1800s to the modern textiles and trade hub it became

things.name: Aulum Kirke
things.description: Beautiful Romanesque church from the 12th century with distinctive frescoes, set in the rolling farmland west of Herning

things.name: Jutland Aquarium
things.description: Family-friendly aquarium displaying North Sea and freshwater fish species native to Jutland, including eels, pike and North Sea flatfish

things.name: Holing Lake
things.description: Scenic recreational lake on Herning's outskirts with walking and cycling trails around its shores, popular for fishing and summer swimming

things.name: Textile Museum (Textilforum)
things.description: Museum dedicated to Herning's identity as the centre of Denmark's textile and clothing industry, with historic looms and fashion exhibitions

things.name: Herning City Centre
things.description: Compact modern town centre with pedestrianised shopping streets, cafes and the large Herning Congress Centre hosting major trade fairs

things.name: Ikast Lakes
things.description: Chain of picturesque glacial lakes near the town of Ikast offering peaceful walking routes and opportunities to spot Danish bird life

things.name: MCH Arena
things.description: Home of FC Midtjylland, Denmark's dominant club and regular Champions League participants, with a 11,800-seat modern stadium
